_help.project: short project name, e.g. "my-app" — shows on the dashboard plaque + tags every log line
_help.mode: 'new' = agents scaffold the folders below from scratch · 'existing' = drive the folders as they already are (no scaffolding)
_help.autonomy: L1 = report only (start here) · L2 = assisted, no merge · L3 = unattended
_help.agent_platform: 'auto' = detect Cursor / Claude Code / Hermes at runtime · or lock to 'cursor' | 'claude' | 'hermes'
_help.agent_models: per-platform model ids — see tools/agent-models.json. Defaults: cursor composer-2.5 · claude sonnet · hermes inherit
_help.agent_model: optional shorthand when agent_platform is not auto (same as agent_models.<platform>)
_help.improvement_policy: conform = match existing style, recommend only · guided = recommend then user picks · auto = apply all recommendations
_help.locale: en = English user-facing text · th = Thai · auto = match the language the user writes in
_help.services: one entry per FE/BE folder — add as many as you want; each can live in its OWN base path (see service.path)
_help.service.id: short unique name you refer to the service by. e.g. "web", "admin", "api", "worker". Used in commands like: zsh tools/scaffold-all.sh api
_help.service.side: "fe" = frontend/UI (owned by fe + fe-anim agents) · "be" = backend/API/logic/data (owned by be + be-sr agents)
_help.service.path: where the code lives. RELATIVE ("web", "apps/admin") = a subfolder UNDER this project root. ABSOLUTE ("/Users/me/work/billing-svc") = anywhere on disk — use this so each service can sit in a DIFFERENT base path / existing repo
_help.service.stack: framework hint for scaffolding. fe: nextjs | vite-react | sveltekit | astro · be: nestjs | fastapi | node-express | go · "" = none / don't scaffold
